title: Dark Gooey Egyptian Saskatoon Berry Tagine
description: A rich and earthy Egyptian dish featuring saskatoon berries, perfect for a cozy meal, with a unique blend of ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients served in a bento box.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the ground cumin and coriander and cook for 1 minute.
Add the saskatoon berries, honey, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the berries have broken down and the mixture is gooey.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired, in a bento box.
Steam will rise from the dish, carrying the aroma of the spices and berries.
Let it cool for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
